cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Moral Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Burnham's initial goal of a clean burglary deteriorates into a profound moral conflict as he confronts the human cost of the heist. From discovering the house is occupied to resisting the murder of the hostages, his internal struggle intensifies, culminating in physical confrontation and self-sacrifice to prevent a gas explosion.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Burnham believes the house is empty and plans a non-violent theft.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Burnham actively opposes his accomplices to protect the hostages, suffering severe burns in the process.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his moral awakening, Burnham remains trapped within the criminal operation and must reconcile his actions with survival.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Isolation and Obsession with the Safe
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After Junior's departure and subsequent murder, Burnham's objective shifts from shared gain to a solitary, obsessive fixation on opening the safe. His determination persists despite abandonment and escalating danger, transforming the heist into a personal mission divorced from its original purpose.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Burnham still operates as part of a team with shared goals.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Burnham is isolated, psychologically strained, and committed to completing the heist alone.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The feasibility of opening the safe without tools or cooperation remains uncertain, and his obsession may be self-destructive.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Psychological Breakdown and Coercion
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Burnham's status evolves from a calculating intruder to a psychologically broken captive under Raoul's control. Frustration over technical failure leads to violent outbursts, which devolve into submission after torture, forcing him to participate in coercion against the hostages.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Burnham is an active problem-solver attempting to regain control of the house systems.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Burnham is traumatized, physically harmed, and coerced into threatening the hostages under duress.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His compliance is not genuine loyalty, leaving open the possibility of resistance despite his subjugated status.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Reckoning and Redemption Attempt
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Faced with Sarah's medical emergency, Burnham abandons the heist to save her life, marking a turning point from criminal to protector. His defiance of Raoul affirms this moral shift, but his attempt to escape ends in death, leaving his redemption tragically incomplete.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Burnham is still aligned with the intruders, focused on completing the heist within a time limit.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Burnham dies trying to flee, having chosen to save a child over completing the crime.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Burnham could have fully escaped his past or been absolved for his earlier actions remains unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Shifting Power Dynamics with Raoul
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Burnham's relationship with Raoul deteriorates from uneasy alliance to victimization and defiance. Initially sidelined and then tortured, Burnham resists by disabling the camera, signaling a break in submission and the collapse of any functional partnership.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Raoul asserts dominance by isolating Burnham, treating him as expendable.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Burnham actively resists Raoul's control, culminating in symbolic and verbal defiance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The confrontation remains unresolved as Raoul's violent authority persists, leaving Burnham's survival in doubt until his final moments.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Overwhelm to Agency: Securing the New Home
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Meg transitions from being overwhelmed by the pressure of securing housing to actively mastering her new environment by arming the security system, asserting control despite emotional strain.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Meg is physically and emotionally strained, facing time pressure and familial tension while touring the last available property.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Meg successfully arms the security system, demonstrating persistence and growing competence in her new home.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her slurred speech and intoxication suggest underlying emotional distress that remains unaddressed despite her technical success.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fracturing and Reclamation of Maternal Strength
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Meg's relationship with Sarah evolves through moments of emotional exposure, conflict, and protective urgency, revealing a strained but deepening bond under crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Meg suppresses grief, leading to a fragile and awkward emotional connection with Sarah.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Meg shifts from relying on external rescue to accepting full responsibility for their survival, anchoring herself through her role as protector.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Emotional wounds and unresolved betrayal continue to affect their dynamic, risking further breakdown under stress.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting Claustrophobia and Psychological Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Meg's internal battle with claustrophobia intensifies into full dissociation, culminating in a hallucinatory escape that is violently disrupted by reality.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Meg's claustrophobia is triggered and concretely established as a critical vulnerability when the panic room door slams shut.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Meg experiences a complete psychological collapse and dissociative episode, only pulled back by Sarah's intervention.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The boundary between reality and dissociation remains dangerously permeable, threatening her ability to survive.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Illusion and Reality of Safety
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The panic room shifts from a symbol of hidden security to a trap of isolation, exposing Meg's false assumptions and evolving understanding of true safety.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: The hidden panic room is discovered, validating Meg's perception and offering a promise of safety.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Meg realizes the panic room has been intentionally sealed by the intruders, transforming it into a prison rather than a refuge.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The room's compromised state leaves them isolated with no reliable means of escape or communication.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Passive Survivor to Active Resistor
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Meg evolves from shock and passivity into decisive action, manipulating both intruders and authorities to maintain control, ultimately leading their escape.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Meg is overwhelmed by the realization of the intrusion, struggling to process the threat.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Meg assumes leadership, deceives the police, and initiates a daring escape, asserting full agency in the crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her manipulative choices and the intruders' awareness of her actions suggest ongoing danger despite her regained control.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Vulnerability to Agency: Sarah's Survival Evolution
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sarah transforms from a passive, unaware victim into an active, resourceful survivor who repeatedly takes decisive action under pressure. Her journey begins with helplessness as she is observed by the intruder, but she gradually assumes leadership—redirecting the elevator, managing Meg's collapse, discovering the ventilation system, and attempting Morse code communication. Each step marks a shift from reactivity to initiative, culminating in her critical intervention to protect Meg's escape.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sarah is unaware and vulnerable, observed by the intruder in her bedroom.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sarah actively diverts Raoul's attention, enabling Meg's escape and asserting control in a high-stakes moment.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fracture and Reconciliation of Mother-Daughter Trust
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sarah's relationship with her mother, Meg, undergoes intense strain and eventual reconnection. Initially forced to care for an incapacitated Meg, Sarah later confronts emotional conflict when she calls out for her father, challenging their unity. The bond is tested further during Sarah's medical crisis, where Meg must act as protector. Ultimately, after surviving trauma, Sarah reestablishes trust by seeking Meg's input on their future, symbolizing emotional recovery.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Meg collapses into dissociation, leaving Sarah isolated and burdened with survival.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sarah and Meg share a peaceful, reflective moment in Central Park, reconnecting and planning their future together.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The lingering impact of Sarah's divided loyalty between her parents remains unexplored.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Escalating Medical Crisis and Fight for Survival
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sarah's underlying medical condition becomes a central, life-threatening conflict. What begins as subtle physical decline escalates into a full-blown hypoglycemic crisis, marked by tremors, disorientation, convulsions, and the terrifying realization that she will die without Glucogen. Her struggle shifts from surviving the intruders to surviving her own body, creating urgency and dependency, and exposing the intruders' indifference to her medical needs.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sarah shows early signs of hypoglycemia—trembling and lip discoloration—with no access to treatment.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sarah remains untreated and in critical condition, explicitly stating she will die without an injection, while others hesitate.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The Glucogen is missing, and no one has administered life-saving intervention, leaving her survival in immediate jeopardy.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Trapped: The Descent into Isolation and Failed Rescue Attempts
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Sarah's status shifts from attempted escape to complete entrapment, followed by a series of failed efforts to break isolation. After failing to retrieve her phone, she is locked in the panic room, cut off from communication. She transitions from hoping for rescue to actively seeking ways to signal for help—shouting, investigating the room's secrets, and using Morse code. Despite her ingenuity, each attempt fails, reinforcing her physical and psychological confinement.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Sarah flees into the panic room, cut off from communication and escape.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Sarah successfully signals a neighbor via Morse code, but he rejects the plea, leaving her trapped and unheard.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: No external help has arrived, and the intruders remain in control, maintaining Sarah's status as isolated and endangered.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Intruder's Gaze and the Unseen Connection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: A mysterious emotional undercurrent connects Sarah to the intruders, particularly evident in the intruder's silent reaction upon seeing her and later in the tension around her medical needs. This arc blends personal threat, emotional ambiguity, and shifting power dynamics—her life becomes a moral dilemma for the intruders, especially when Burnham hesitates to act. The initial personal connection hinted at in the bedroom resurfaces in moments where her survival is weighed against the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: The intruder enters Sarah's bedroom and reacts emotionally, suggesting a personal history or connection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: The intruders are aware Sarah will die without treatment, yet hesitate to help, indicating internal conflict possibly rooted in prior knowledge or feeling.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The nature of the intruder's emotional connection to Sarah remains unexplained, leaving motives and potential backstory unresolved.
